articles.title: LEDGER CUSTOMERS ALERTED TO NEW DATA EXPOSURE VIA PAYMENT PROCESSOR
articles.summary: Ledger warned customers that a third-party payments provider, Global-e, suffered a data exposure that included customer names and contact details. Ledger has not publicly detailed the breach or its scope. The incident revives concern about how crypto firms and their vendors protect user data after Ledger's 2020 leak and ensuing legal fallout.

articles.title: GLOBAL ETP FLOWS NEAR 2024 RECORD AS BITCOIN LOSES SHARE
articles.summary: CoinShares reports $47.2 billion of inflows into crypto ETPs in 2025, just 3% shy of 2024's record. This was largely driven by a 35% pullback from Bitcoin funds to $26.9 billion while investors rotated $12.7 billion into ETH (up 138%), $3.7 billion into XRP, and $3.6 billion into Solana. The US is still dominant with $42.5 billion of inflows, Europe and Canada are increasing their participation, and there's a strong start to 2026, showing weekly flows around $582 million.

articles.title: ETHEREUM'S BLOCKCHAIN TRILEMMA SOLUTION
articles.summary: Ethereum has achieved a breakthrough combination of decentralization, consensus, and high bandwidth through PeerDAS (live on mainnet) and ZKEVMs (production-quality performance, pending final safety work). This solves a fundamental trilemma in decentralized networks, representing the culmination of 10 years of research. The Ethereum roadmap calls for gas limit increases and first ZKEVM node deployments in 2026, with ZKEVMs becoming the primary block validation method by 2027-2030.

articles.title: ETHEREUM'S PURPOSE: RESILIENCE, NOT CONVENIENCE
articles.summary: Ethereum's core mission is to maximize resilience and digital sovereignty, not to chase marginal efficiency or convenience gains. It prioritizes these factors to ensure users retain access and control even if cloud providers, developers, or corporations fail or censor, making decentralized, permissionless blockspace a safeguard against catastrophic loss and unequal dependence.

articles.title: THE DOWNFALL OF TGES IN 2025
articles.summary: 84.7% of 118 major 2025 TGEs now trade below launch valuations, with median FDV collapsing 71% and median MC down 67%. High-profile projects like Berachain, Nillion, and Mira suffered 90%+ drawdowns within months. The VC-driven “low float, high FDV” model created pump-and-dump schemes where teams raised at $20M-$50M seed rounds, listed at $1B+ FDVs, and dumped on retail. The 15.3% survivors shared three traits: organic demand in sectors like RWA (185% YTD ROI), payments, and restaking; reasonable entry valuations under $200M FDV; and institutional alignment as infrastructure layers, stablecoin issuers, and L2s with consistent transaction fees.

articles.title: OPEN BANKING VS. CARD PAYMENTS: CAN PISPS DISRUPT THE CARD STACK?
articles.summary: Traditional card payments rely on a complex four-party model that routes transactions through acquirers, card schemes, and issuers, with fees distributed across each layer. Open banking proposes a simpler flow, where Payment Initiation Service Providers (PISPs) can initiate payments directly from a user's bank account with consent. While this could bypass card schemes and reshape the payment value chain, card networks still provide large-scale clearing and settlement, meaning open banking payments may ultimately replicate similar batch-settlement dynamics rather than fully replacing cards.

articles.title: 2026 PREDICTIONS
articles.summary: DeFi TVL will surpass 2025's $101B peak while Solana validator count drops to ~500, and Ethereum scales mainnet but won't meaningfully bring users home from L2s, with at least 3 L2s shutting down due to no revenue. The stablecoin landscape will shift as USDT/USDC dominance falls below 70% with boutique offerings gaining share, and Polymarket will announce its own chain while Hyperliquid reclaims 50% OI dominance. Market structure will evolve with P/E ratios becoming accepted valuation metrics, exposing blockchain tokens as overvalued, as value shifts toward applications.

articles.title: CREATOR COINS AND THE TIMELINE PROBLEM
articles.summary: The Base app creator coins debate mirrors a familiar pattern: foundations push new user acquisition metas that fracture communities when OGs feel value isn't flowing to their projects. The core insight is that creator coins solve the right problem (turning attention into assets) but in the wrong container – timelines reward loudness and engagement farming rather than signal. Base could pivot from a creator-centric to a topic-centric model, essentially building a “Bloomberg Terminal for culture” that organizes around trends rather than individual creators.

articles.title: SOLANA ONCHAIN VOLUME OVERTAKES MAJOR CEXS
articles.summary: Solana's share of total crypto trading volume has surged from just 1% in 2022 to 12% in 2025.

articles.title: STATE OF X402: AI AGENT PAYMENTS AT SCALE
articles.summary: x402 protocol activity in December revealed 63 million AI agent micropayment transactions totaling $7.5M in USDC across 1,100+ projects.

articles.title: NEOBANKS WILL FUEL ETHEREUM'S 2026 GROWTH
articles.summary: Ethereum's 2026 growth will be driven by crypto-native neobanking products that combine yield, self-custody, and onchain financial services familiar to everyday users.

articles.title: STRATEGY STARTS 2026 WITH ANOTHER $116M BITCOIN BUY
articles.summary: Strategy purchased an additional 1,286 BTC for $116.3 million, bringing its total holdings to 673,783 BTC, more than 3% of Bitcoin's fixed supply, while also boosting its USD reserve to $2.25 billion to strengthen liquidity.
